steps.title: Use the horizontal shift
steps.content: Data set Q adds $540$ to every $x$-value from data set P. This shifts the scatterplot and its line of best fit $540$ units to the right. The slope stays the same.

steps.title: Shift a readable point
steps.content: A readable point on the original best-fit line is about $(100,14)$. After the shift, that point becomes $(640,14)$.

steps.title: Check the shifted model
steps.content:
The model with the matching slope should pass near $(640,14)$. For the candidate with slope $\frac{35}{1{,}301}$ and intercept $-3$:
$$
-3+\frac{35}{1{,}301}(640)=14.2\text{ approximately}
$$
This is near the shifted point, while the same slope with intercept $26$ is much too high.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the candidate lines and mark the shifted point. The line with intercept $-3$ and slope $\frac{35}{1{,}301}$ is the one that stays near $(640,14)$. The answer is A.
